overview: The Graphic Autonumber solution is a Microsoft Power Platform application (Power Apps canvas app + Power Automate flows + Dataverse + SharePoint) embedded as a tab in deal-specific Microsoft Teams channels. Primary actors are the Submitter (deal team member or Graphic Designer), the Graphic Designer (production FTE, 262 across Growth Markets/EMEA/Americas), and the Reviewer/Author-SME. The end-to-end journey: a deal team creates a Graphic Tracker for a Teams channel (capturing Opportunity ID, Deal Name, channel link), users add line items that are auto-stamped with a read-only Graphic Control Number in the format `<OppID>.<YY>.<Order>` (e.g., 000007.26.0001), notifications fire to the Designer on submission and 'Updates Needed', and to the Submitter/Author on 'Completed'. BT-confirmed scale: ~58,050 graphics/month with 10–20% FY25 growth, ≤300 concurrent users, and no peak spikes — easily absorbed by the low-code platform.

architectureStyle.rationale: BT confirmed (Q3) the solution is built on Microsoft Power Platform integrating Teams, Dataverse and SharePoint. BT (Q4) confirmed ≤300 users with no peak spikes and 'simple action items by generating numbers and sending emails — no complicated logic or APIs', so a custom microservice/event-driven backend would be over-engineering. Power Apps + Dataverse + Power Automate natively delivers the Teams-embedded UI, transactional autonumber storage, RBAC against Teams membership (Q5: 'Team and Channel dropdowns display only Teams/Channels user is part of'), and Outlook email notifications without bespoke infrastructure.

components.name: GraphicTrackerCanvasApp
components.responsibility: Power Apps canvas application embedded as a tab in each deal Teams channel. Renders Landing, Create Tracker, Open Existing Tracker, View Tracker, New Item, and Edit Item screens. Enforces read-only display of the Graphic Control Number and personalised 'Hello <username>' greeting.

components.name: AutonumberFlow
components.responsibility: Power Automate cloud flow triggered on Dataverse row create for a GraphicRequest. Atomically allocates the next 4-digit Order within the (OppId, Year) scope and writes back the formatted GraphicControlNumber. Also triggers the New-Graphic email to the Designer.

components.name: StatusChangeFlow
components.responsibility: Power Automate flow triggered on GraphicRequest.Status modification. Routes 'Updates Needed' emails to the Designer and 'Completed' emails to the Submitter/Author. Respects the per-item 'Email status updates to Author' toggle.

components.name: DataverseGraphicStore
components.responsibility: System of record for GraphicTracker, GraphicRequest, and OrderSequence tables. Provides ACID transactions, row-level security, and the autonumber column.

components.name: SharePointAssetStore
components.responsibility: Stores completed graphic files, templates, and production materials per deal. Access restricted to assigned deal users; link surfaced in the Power App to guide folder/naming conventions.

components.name: NotificationService
components.responsibility: Power Automate 'Send an email (V2)' Outlook connector. Renders the three PDD email templates (New Graphics, Updates Needed, Completed) and dispatches via Exchange Online.

components.name: TeamsMembershipResolver
components.responsibility: Microsoft Graph / Teams connector that filters Team and Channel dropdowns to only those the current user is a member of (BT business rule from Q5).

components.name: IdentityProvider
components.responsibility: Azure AD / Entra ID single sign-on. Supplies the signed-in user identity used for 'Submitted by' auto-fill, the 'Hello <username>' greeting, and Teams membership checks.

entities.name: GraphicTracker
entities.purpose: One tracker per deal/opportunity, scoped to a Teams channel.

fields.name: OpportunityId
fields.notes: 6-digit Opp ID from assignment note; immutable after creation

fields.name: TeamsChannelLink
fields.notes: Deep link to the deal Teams channel

fields.name: TeamId
fields.notes: Azure AD group / Team object id

entities.name: GraphicRequest
entities.purpose: One row per graphic line item logged in a tracker. Source of GraphicControlNumber.

fields.name: GraphicControlNumber
fields.notes: Format <OppId>.<YY>.<Order> e.g., 000007.26.0001. Read-only after generation (BT Q2). Populated by AutonumberFlow.

fields.name: Status
fields.notes: Values: New | In Progress | Updates Needed | Completed. Default 'New'.

fields.name: Section
fields.notes: Section number in the Word response

fields.name: SubmittedBy
fields.notes: FK → SystemUser. Auto-filled from current user.

fields.name: GraphicDesigner
fields.notes: FK → SystemUser; assigned by deal team

fields.name: SourceFileControlNumber
fields.notes: Optional reference to parent graphic when modifying a reusable asset

fields.name: EmailStatusUpdatesToAuthor
fields.notes: Toggle from Edit Item screen. Default true.

entities.name: OrderSequence
entities.purpose: Atomic counter that allocates the 4-digit Order portion of the autonumber, scoped per Opportunity+Year to keep numbering chronological and consecutive across designers (PDD pre-process rule).

fields.name: LastOrder
fields.notes: Last allocated order, 0–9999. PDD assumption: ≤9000 graphics per deal.

dataModel.sequenceAllocation: On GraphicRequest create, AutonumberFlow runs synchronously and performs an atomic 'get-or-create + increment' on the OrderSequence row whose SequenceKey = <OppId>.<YY>. The increment uses Dataverse's pessimistic update with optimistic-concurrency retry (If-Match ETag): read LastOrder, compute LastOrder+1, conditional update; on 412 Precondition Failed retry up to 5x with jitter. The new order is left-padded to 4 digits and concatenated as `<OppId>.<YY>.<Order>` into GraphicRequest.GraphicControlNumber, which carries a Unique constraint as a second-line collision guard. Scope is (OpportunityId, Year) — not global and not per-tracker — so reruns and parallel designers always pull the next consecutive number for that deal-year. The field is sealed read-only after first write (BT Q2).

userFlows.name: New Graphic Request Submission & Autonumber Generation
steps: 1. Submitter opens the Graphic Tracker tab in the deal Teams channel — GraphicTrackerCanvasApp loads with 'Hello <username>'.
2. Submitter clicks 'Add/Update requests in existing Graphics Tracker' and lands on the view-tracker screen for the deal.
3. Submitter clicks '+ New Item' → new-item screen renders. Submitted By auto-fills from IdentityProvider.
4. Submitter fills Title, Graphics Type, Instructions, Section, Author/SME, Designer, leaves 'Email status updates to Author' = ON, and clicks Save.
5. GraphicTrackerCanvasApp writes a GraphicRequest row to DataverseGraphicStore with Status='New' and GraphicControlNumber=blank.
6. AutonumberFlow fires on row-create, atomically increments OrderSequence for key '<OppId>.<YY>', formats '<OppId>.<YY>.<NNNN>' and updates the GraphicRequest row.
7. AutonumberFlow then calls NotificationService to send the 'New Graphics for Proposal' email to the Graphic Designer.
8. GraphicTrackerCanvasApp auto-refreshes; the new line item appears in view-tracker with the read-only Graphic Control Number populated.
userFlows.asciiSequence:
Submitter           CanvasApp          Dataverse           AutonumberFlow      NotificationSvc     Designer
   |                    |                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--Open Teams tab--->|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |                    |--auth (Entra)---->|                       |                    |               |
   |<--Hello <user>-----|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--+ New Item------->|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--Fill & Save------>|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |                    |--Create Row------>|                       |                    |               |
   |                    |                   |--Row.Created event--->|                    |               |
   |                    |                   |<--Read OrderSeq-------|                    |               |
   |                    |                   |--Increment (ETag)---->|                    |               |
   |                    |                   |<--Patch GraphReq------|                    |               |
   |                    |                   |                       |--Send email------->|               |
   |                    |                   |                       |                    |--Inbox------->|
   |                    |<--Auto-refresh----|                       |                    |               |
   |<--CtrlNum shown----|                   |                       |                    |               |

userFlows.name: Status Transition: Updates Needed → Designer Email
steps: 1. Reviewer opens the tracker and selects an existing item → edit-item screen.
2. Reviewer changes Status from 'In Progress' to 'Updates Needed' and clicks Save.
3. GraphicTrackerCanvasApp updates the GraphicRequest row in DataverseGraphicStore.
4. StatusChangeFlow fires on the Status field change, builds the 'Updates Needed for Graphics' email (including Graphic Control Number and Submitted By) and dispatches via NotificationService.
5. The Graphic Designer receives the email; if 'Email status updates to Author' is ON, the Author/SME also receives a status update.
userFlows.asciiSequence:
Reviewer            CanvasApp          Dataverse           StatusChangeFlow    NotificationSvc     Designer/Author
   |--Edit Item------->|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--Status=Updates-->|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--Save------------>|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |                   |--Update Row------>|                       |                    |               |
   |                   |                   |--Status changed------>|                    |               |
   |                   |                   |                       |--Render template-->|               |
   |                   |                   |                       |                    |--Email------->|

userFlows.name: Completion Notification to Submitter
steps: 1. Designer opens the item, sets Status='Completed', Save.
3. StatusChangeFlow detects Completed transition, fetches all completed-row metadata, and renders the 'Graphics Completed for Proposal' email.
4. NotificationService emails the Submitter; Author/SME receives a copy if the per-item toggle is ON.
userFlows.asciiSequence:
Designer            CanvasApp          Dataverse           StatusChangeFlow    NotificationSvc     Submitter
   |--Status=Complete->|                   |                       |                    |               |
   |--Save------------>|--Update Row------>|                       |                    |               |
   |                   |                   |--Status changed------>|                    |               |
   |                   |                   |                       |--Render Completed->|               |
   |                   |                   |                       |                    |--Email------->|

techStack.frontend: Power Apps canvas app embedded as a Teams tab — BT (Q3) confirmed Power Platform; canvas app gives pixel-level control of the PDD-specified Accenture-themed UI and the Hello-username/landing/three-option layout.
techStack.backend: Power Automate cloud flows (AutonumberFlow, StatusChangeFlow) — BT (Q4) said the logic is simple (generate numbers, send emails); Power Automate eliminates custom service code.
techStack.database: Microsoft Dataverse for GraphicTracker / GraphicRequest / OrderSequence (ACID, ETag concurrency, RLS); SharePoint Online for finished-graphic file storage and templates per the PDD.
techStack.infrastructure: Microsoft 365 tenant (Accenture) — no separate hosting needed; flows run on shared Power Platform capacity, well within 300-user / ~58k-events/month limits.
techStack.identity: Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) SSO with Teams + Power Apps; Teams membership drives the Team/Channel dropdown filter and tab access.

integrations.purpose: Host the Power App as a channel tab; resolve user's Team/Channel memberships for dropdowns.
integrations.payloadSummary: Teams tab manifest; Graph calls returning the user's Team and Channel lists.

integrations.purpose: Persist trackers, requests, and the OrderSequence counter; raise row-create/row-update events to flows.
integrations.payloadSummary: CRUD on GraphicTracker / GraphicRequest / OrderSequence rows; change-data events.

integrations.purpose: Provide the link in the canvas app to deal-specific folders and the naming-convention guide; store completed graphic files.
integrations.payloadSummary: Document library URLs; optional file metadata reads.

integrations.purpose: Send the three notification emails (New Graphics, Updates Needed, Completed).
integrations.payloadSummary: HTML email body with the PDD-specified tables and subjects.

integrations.purpose: Authenticate the user and supply identity (display name, UPN) used for 'Submitted By' and Hello banner.

notifications.trigger: GraphicRequest created (Status='New') — fired by AutonumberFlow after the control number is assigned.
notifications.bodyTemplate:
Dear Graphic Designer,

A new Graphic request has been submitted.

| ID | Title | Status | Graphics Type | Instructions | Section | Author/SME/Contact | Submitted By | Graphic Designer |
|----|-------|--------|----------------|---------------|---------|---------------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1  | Overview | New | Medium | This is demo. | 1 | Kuldeep | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa |
| 2  | Facts    | New | Basic  | This is demo. | 3 | Kuldeep | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa |

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to the Submitter.
notifications.userOptOut: Per-item toggle 'Email status updates to Author' on edit-item / new-item screen controls Author copies; Designer notification is mandatory for the autonumbering completion signal per BT Q7.

notifications.trigger: GraphicRequest.Status changed to 'Updates Needed' — fired by StatusChangeFlow.
notifications.bodyTemplate:
Dear Graphic Designer,

Updates needed request has been submitted for the below Graphic request

| ID | Title | Status | Graphics Type | Instructions | Section | Graphic Control Number | Submitted By | Graphic Designer |
|----|-------|--------|----------------|---------------|---------|--------------------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 1  | Overview | Updates Needed | Medium | This is demo. | 1 | 12345678.25.0001 | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa |
| 2  | Facts    | Updates Needed | Basic  | This is demo. | 3 | 12345678.25.0002 | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa |

If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to the Submitter.
notifications.userOptOut: Edit-item screen → 'Email status updates to Author' toggle.

notifications.trigger: GraphicRequest.Status changed to 'Completed' — fired by StatusChangeFlow.
notifications.bodyTemplate:
Dear [Submitted By],

We are pleased to inform you that the graphics for your proposal have been completed. Below are the details of the completed graphics:

| ID | Title | Graphic Control Number | Status | Graphics Type | Instructions | Source File Control Number | Section | Author/SME/Contact | Submitted By | Graphic Designer | Created Date | Modified Date | Modified by |
|----|-------|--------------------------|--------|----------------|---------------|------------------------------|---------|---------------------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|----------------|---------------|
| 1  | Overview | 12345678.25.0001 | Completed | Medium | This is demo. | 87654321.24.0023 | 1 | Kuldeep | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa | default | default | Kuldeep |
| 2  | Facts    | 12345678.25.0002 | Completed | Basic  | This is demo. | - | 3 | Kuldeep | Kuldeep Garud | Ramanujam, Deepa | default | default | Kuldeep |

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to the graphic design team.
If you require any modifications to the completed graphics, you can submit an Updates Needed request. Please provide the necessary details and instructions for the modifications.

Thank you for your submission and collaboration.

Note: This is an automatically generated email, please do not reply.
notifications.userOptOut: Edit-item screen → 'Email status updates to Author' toggle suppresses the Author/SME copy; Submitter receives the email regardless.

nonFunctional.scalability: BT Q4: ≤300 concurrent users, ~58,050 requests/month (~2,640/business day, ~330/business hour, ~5.5/minute peak). With FY25 growth of 10–20% the upper bound is ~70,000/month — still well within standard Power Platform per-flow throughput (5,000 runs/5min) and Dataverse API limits. OrderSequence is the only contended row; scoping by (OppId, Year) means contention is per-deal-per-year, typically ≤9000 lifetime per BT assumption, so write-rate per key is negligible.
nonFunctional.security: Entra ID SSO for all users. RBAC: tab visibility requires Teams channel membership; Team/Channel dropdowns filtered to memberships (BT Q5). Dataverse row-level security on GraphicTracker by TeamId; GraphicRequest inherits via TrackerId. GraphicControlNumber is field-level read-only after first write (BT Q2) — enforced at the canvas-app form, the Power Automate write rule, and a Dataverse plugin/business rule to block any direct API edit. SharePoint link surfaced in the app but actual file access governed by SharePoint permissions per deal (PDD).
nonFunctional.performance: Target: autonumber assignment + UI confirmation ≤2s p95; email dispatch ≤30s p95. Synchronous Dataverse upsert + ETag retry on OrderSequence keeps end-to-end under 1s in steady state. PDD AHT of 2 minutes is dominated by the human design step, not autonumbering.
nonFunctional.reliability: Target availability 99.5% (aligned with Microsoft 365 SLA). Failure mode BT named explicitly (Q5): Power Automate platform outage. Mitigations: (a) AutonumberFlow runs with retry policy (exponential, 4 retries) and a dead-letter queue; (b) if the flow does not stamp a number within 60s, the canvas app shows an inline banner 'Control number pending — will appear automatically' so the row is still saved and the number backfills when the platform recovers; (c) unique constraint on GraphicControlNumber guards against double-stamp during retry.
nonFunctional.observability: BT Q6 said audit/logging is not required. Design therefore relies on the built-in Dataverse audit log (enabled on GraphicRequest only — Status, GraphicControlNumber fields) and Power Automate run history for the two flows. No custom dashboard built; we flag this as a residual risk below.

pddVisualReferences.whatItShows: Sample Graphic Control Number illustrating the naming convention OppID.YY.Order (e.g., 000007.26.0001) referenced in PDD section 6.4.1.
pddVisualReferences.howUsedInDesign: Drives the GraphicRequest.GraphicControlNumber field format, the OrderSequence allocation key (<OppId>.<YY>), the read-only display on the new-item and edit-item screens, and the validation regex on Source File Control Number.

risks.risk: Autonumber collision under concurrent designers despite optimistic-concurrency retries — could break the 'consecutive in chronological order' rule.
risks.mitigation: Single OrderSequence row per (OppId, Year) with Dataverse ETag-based optimistic locking, 5x retry with jitter, plus Unique constraint on GraphicControlNumber as a backstop; any duplicate raises a flow alert.

risks.risk: Modified/revised graphics: BT (Q2) said the number is read-only and 'auto populates everywhere'. No versioning means lineage between a parent and a revised file is not tracked — could complicate harvesting/reuse.
risks.mitigation: Use the optional 'Source File Control Number' field on edit-item to capture the parent control number when a graphic is modified from a reusable asset; surface it in the Completed email (already shown in PDD template).

risks.risk: Power Automate platform outage stops autonumber stamping (the one failure mode BT explicitly named in Q5).
risks.mitigation: Row save is decoupled from autonumber stamping: the GraphicRequest is persisted with Status='New' and a pending placeholder; flow retries on platform recovery and back-fills the number; user sees a 'Control number pending' banner — no data loss.

risks.risk: No audit trail or RBAC reporting (BT Q6 declined). For a 262-FTE, multi-market process, lack of audit logs may surface later as a compliance/harvesting gap.
risks.mitigation: Enable Dataverse field-level auditing on GraphicRequest.Status and GraphicRequest.GraphicControlNumber at zero build cost — provides a recoverable trail without a custom dashboard, and can be exposed later via a Power BI report if requirements change.

risks.risk: Per-deal cap assumption (≤9000 graphics) gives only 1000 headroom against the 4-digit Order field — a single mega-deal could exhaust the sequence.
risks.mitigation: Add a soft alert on OrderSequence.LastOrder ≥ 9000 to notify the BT lead; field width can be widened to 5 digits with a backwards-compatible migration if breached.

risks.risk: Notification scope ambiguity — BT Q7 confirmed only the Completion email template, while the PDD also defines New Graphics and Updates Needed templates. Risk of building beyond/below BT's stated scope.
risks.mitigation: All three PDD templates implemented in StatusChangeFlow/AutonumberFlow as designed; per-item 'Email status updates to Author' toggle gives the user the on/off control BT described. Confirm with BT in TDD review before build.
